abstract_inverted_index: Vision-Language Pre-training (VLP) has advanced the performance for many vision-language tasks. However, most existing pre-trained models only excel in either understanding-based tasks or generation-based Furthermore, improvement been largely achieved by scaling up dataset with noisy image-text pairs collected from web, which is a suboptimal source of supervision. In this paper, we propose BLIP, new VLP framework transfers flexibly to both understanding and generation BLIP effectively utilizes web data bootstrapping captions, where captioner generates synthetic captions filter removes ones. We achieve state-of-the-art results on wide range tasks, such as retrieval (+2.7% average recall@1), image captioning (+2.8% CIDEr), VQA (+1.6% score). also demonstrates strong generalization ability when directly transferred video-language zero-shot manner.
